msg: PLANNED iter002_ws1p2c_tm128: Switch the wide-N kernel from 1p1c 64x128 to a ws1p2c 128x128 warp-specialized tile so two consumer warpgroups can drive more WGMMA issue per CTA on this compute-bound shape.

PLANNED iter003_wn160: Increase the 1p1c kernel WARP_N from 128 to 160 so each CTA performs more WGMMA work per producer load and better amortizes barrier and TMA overhead on this compute-bound wide-N shape.
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1186 lines)
Custom SASS extracted for iter001 (1067 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter001 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_2048x16384x2048/gpt-5/sass_compare_iter001.json)

PLANNED iter004_stmatrix: Enable the stmatrix Choreo flag on the proven 1p1c base so codegen uses more baseline-like shared-memory matrix staging, targeting the SASS gaps in LDS or STS usage and the much higher synchronization density.

PLANNED iter005_tilek128: Increase TILE_K from 64 to 128 on the proven 1p1c base so each producer load feeds twice as many WGMMA steps, cutting barrier traffic per flop while staying within H800 shared-memory limits.

PLANNED iter005_persistent_1p1c: Convert the proven 1p1c wide-N base to persistent scheduling so each SM reuses the same warp-specialized pipeline across multiple tiles, reducing launch churn and improving producer-consumer steady state on a low-occupancy compute-bound kernel.

PLANNED iter006_launchbounds: Keep the proven 1p1c 64x128 kernel unchanged at the .co level and add __launch_bounds__(256, 2) in the generated CUDA so Hopper schedules for two resident CTAs per SM more aggressively on this low-occupancy compute-bound kernel.

PLANNED iter007_fastmath: Keep the new launch_bounds(256,2) best kernel intact and add nvcc --use_fast_math so the fp16-to-fp32 warp-specialized path gets more aggressive math lowering without changing tiling or synchronization.

PLANNED iter008_unroll: Keep the launch_bounds(256,2) best kernel intact and explicitly unroll the 4-step WGMMA inner loop in generated CUDA so the compiler emits a tighter instruction schedule for the fixed TILE_K=64 microkernel.

PLANNED iter009_arrive_once: Keep the launch_bounds(256,2) best kernel intact but move warpgroup_arrive out of the 4-step WGMMA micro-loop so each K-stage opens the warpgroup once instead of four times, directly targeting the barrier-density gap versus baseline.

PLANNED iter010_arrive_unroll: Keep iter009 with once-per-stage warpgroup_arrive placement and explicitly unroll the fixed 4-step WGMMA micro-loop so the cleaner synchronization pattern also exposes a fully straight-line issue sequence.

PLANNED iter011_arrive_fastmath: Keep iter009 with once-per-stage warpgroup_arrive placement and add nvcc --use_fast_math so the cleaner synchronization base can test whether math contraction helps the remaining compute-side issue overhead.

PLANNED iter012_blockswizzle: Keep iter011 with launch_bounds, once-per-stage warpgroup_arrive, and fast math, but remap CTAs through a small swizzled block schedule so TMA loads and output stores see a more locality-friendly tile order on this wide-N shape.

PLANNED iter013_l2promo: Keep iter011 with launch-bounds, arrive-once, and fast-math compute pipeline unchanged, but promote the two input TMA tensor maps to L2_256B so repeated wide-N tile fetches have a better chance to persist in cache.

PLANNED iter014_lb3: Keep iter013s arrive-once, fast-math, and input L2 promotion path unchanged, but tighten launch bounds from 256x2 to 256x3 so ptxas is forced to trade some instruction-level parallelism for higher residency on this 128-register kernel.

PLANNED iter015_rreg120: Keep iter013 with launch-bounds 256x2, arrive-once, fast-math, and input L2 promotion unchanged, but cap ptxas at 120 registers so the kernel gives up a small amount of ILP in exchange for potentially better residency without the impossible 256x3 target.

PLANNED iter016_hoist: Keep iter013s launch-bounds, arrive-once, fast-math, and input L2 promotion path unchanged, but enable Choreo hoist-offset and hoist-scale so address-generation work is lifted out of the hot loop and integer issue pressure drops.

PLANNED iter017_ptxbar: Keep iter013s launch-bounds, arrive-once, fast-math, and input L2 promotion path unchanged, but enable Choreo ptx-barrier so the CTA synchronization path uses lighter PTX barriers and may reduce remaining compute-side barrier overhead.

PLANNED iter018_wait1: Keep iter013s launch-bounds, arrive-once, fast-math, and input L2 promotion path unchanged, but move WGMMA completion waiting to warpgroup_wait<1>() inside the K-loop and leave warpgroup_wait<0>() only after the loop so TMA of tile K+1 can overlap with the tail of tile K compute.

PLANNED iter019_splitarrive: Keep iter018s wait1-overlap pipeline unchanged, but move empty[stage].arrive() before warpgroup_wait<1>() so the producer warp is released as soon as shared-memory reads are dispatched instead of waiting for the partial WGMMA drain.

PLANNED iter020_wait1_ptxbar: Keep iter018s wait1-overlap pipeline intact, but regenerate the base with --ptx-barrier so the same overlapped producer-consumer schedule uses lighter PTX-backed CTA barriers and may shave the remaining synchronization cost that plain wait1 did not remove.

PLANNED iter021_split_epilogue: Keep iter020s wait1+ptxbar pipeline intact, but split the 64x128 output shared store into two 64x64 store_fragment_d calls over the same 128-stride tile so shared-store bank conflicts drop without changing the final 128-wide TMA store-to-global path.

PLANNED iter022_dual_tma_epilogue: Keep iter021 wait1+ptxbar intact, but replace the shared 64x128 output tile with two independent 64x64 output buffers and two 64-wide TMA shared-to-global stores so the remaining epilogue shared-store bank conflicts drop further.

PLANNED iter023_wait2: Keep iter021 split-epilogue as the active best, but deepen the overlapped main-loop drain from warpgroup_wait<1>() to warpgroup_wait<2>() so the three-stage pipeline exposes more producer-consumer overlap and reduces CTA-barrier-driven no-eligible cycles.

PLANNED iter024_prearrive_wait2: Keep iter023 wait2 split-epilogue as the active best, but pre-arrive the next full barrier before its TMA completes so the next stage can close earlier while current WGMMA work is still in flight, reducing CTA-barrier-driven no-eligible cycles without changing the output path.

PLANNED iter025_kunroll32_wait2: Keep iter023 wait2 split-epilogue as the active best, but explicitly unroll the 32-step outer K loop so nvcc can schedule a larger steady-state instruction window around the existing wait2 overlap without changing the synchronization or output path.
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1186 lines)

PLANNED iter026_padded_repack: Keep iter023 wait2 split-epilogue as the active best, but store the two 64x64 output fragments into padded 64x65 shared tiles and then repack them into the existing contiguous 64x128 output tile before the single TMA store, targeting the remaining 10-way shared-store bank conflicts without paying the dual-TMA overhead.
Baseline SASS extracted via ncu (1186 lines)
Custom SASS extracted for iter023_wait2 (987 lines)
SASS comparison complete for iter023_wait2 (saved to tuning/sm90_NVIDIA_H800_PCIe/croqtile/perf/matmul_f16fp32_2048x16384x2048/gpt-5/sass_compare_iter023_wait2.json)

PLANNED iter027_regctrl_lb1: Keep iter023 wait2 split-epilogue as the active best, but switch to launch_bounds(256,1) and trade registers explicitly with setmaxnreg so the producer runs lean while the consumer gets a larger accumulator budget, testing whether higher per-warp ILP can overcome the current high barrier density.

PLANNED iter028_lb1_only: Keep iter023 wait2 split-epilogue as the active best and change only launch_bounds from (256,2) to (256,1), testing whether a larger compiler register budget without setmaxnreg can raise consumer ILP enough to offset the lower CTA residency.

PLANNED iter029_stages2_wait1: Keep iter023 split-epilogue topology and launch_bounds(256,2) but reduce pipeline depth from 3 stages to 2 and use an in-loop wait1 drain, testing whether lower barrier traffic and smaller shared staging can increase eligible warps on this barrier-limited kernel.

PLANNED iter030_padded_single_tma: Keep iter023 wait2 overlap and single shared-to-global TMA path, but pad the shared output tile to 64x129 and store only the leading 64x128 view, testing whether bank conflicts can drop without the repack overhead that hurt iter026.
